text.title: Trunk-Based Feature Flags Failure Modes
text.summary: Four antipatterns the spec validator detects. Each has symptom (observable in spec or code), root-cause, and fix.

antipattern.symptom: flag exists in service AND last toggle > 90 days ago AND cleanup_ticket_id is closed or missing.
antipattern.root-cause: Cleanup ticket was never filed at creation, or owner left the team and no one inherited.
antipattern.fix: Nightly aging scan emits a list; quarterly cleanup batch closes them in pairs (author + reviewer). Block new flag creation by owners with active zombies above quota (typical: 3).

antipattern.symptom: owner_email matches a team alias pattern (engineering@, dev@, ops@) or is missing.
antipattern.root-cause: "It's a team flag" sounds collaborative; in practice no one owns cleanup.
antipattern.fix: Reject team aliases in the validator. Pick a primary owner explicitly; that owner can delegate but the spec retains a named human.

antipattern.symptom: Code contains `is_enabled(..., default=True)` checked into trunk; CI does not override.
antipattern.root-cause: Developer flips default during local-dev and forgets to revert. The flag becomes effectively dead — always-on.
antipattern.fix: CI env sets `FLAGS_DEFAULT=off` so any hardcoded default=True surfaces as a failing test. Lint rule greps for the literal pattern.

antipattern.symptom: kill_switch_tested=true in spec but no runbook artefact (recorded action, test run id) backs it.
antipattern.root-cause: Author checks the box without performing the test.
antipattern.fix: Validator requires kill_switch_test_run_id; bot looks it up in CI runs. Quarterly drill: re-test every active flag's kill switch.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: AI-generated flag specs may "complete" missing fields with plausible-but-fake values (synthesized owner emails, fictional ticket ids). The validator MUST verify each external reference (cleanup_ticket_id resolves, owner_email is a real account) before accepting.
